text:
Market today: Rotation is the word - money is shifting out of high-beta tech/semis into other sectors. That's a headwind for your entire book. Chips are getting hit specifically (Sandisk, Micron mentioned sinking), and cloud names like CoreWeave are under pressure from Meta competition fears. Your kind of names are in the crosshairs today.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the loudest problem - it's a 3x levered semi ETF decaying against you, and your stop at $200 isn't far. On margin, a bad day here hurts triple. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, and already near its tight stop at $21.38 - one move away from getting stopped out. SMH (-2.5%) is the underlying pressure confirming semis are weak broadly. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: Whether SMH holds above $540 stop zone, and whether the rotation trade deepens or fades by close.

Bottom line: Protect capital today - your leveraged names are bleeding and rotation is working against your entire book.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - if price approaches, don't hesitate. On 3x leverage plus margin, fighting a down-rotation day is expensive.
SPAL: Stop $21.38 is inches away right now. Consider tightening or exiting manually before it gets hit - new leveraged IPOs move violently.
No new buys today - rotation is actively hurting your sector and adding exposure into weakness on margin is the wrong move.
